title: hieracium pannosum subsp. taygeteum
intro: Hieracium pannosum subsp. taygeteum, commonly known as mountain hawk's beard, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its hairy stems and yellow flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Greek medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Hieracium pannosum subsp. taygeteum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are thought to help protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
